name: Vector Algebra — Arrows with Size and Direction
whatIsIt: A VECTOR has both a size (MAGNITUDE) and a DIRECTION — think of an arrow. In components a vector is written (a, b) or (a, b, c). Its magnitude is |(a, b)| = √(a² + b²). You ADD vectors component by component: (1, 2) + (3, 4) = (4, 6). The DOT PRODUCT a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ gives a single number; it is 0 exactly when the vectors are perpendicular.
whyNeeded: Forces, velocities, displacements and fields all have direction — plain numbers can't describe them. Vectors are the language of physics, engineering, navigation, and 3D graphics.
realLifeUses: A plane's velocity plus the wind
Displacement — how far and which way

vocabulary.meaning: A quantity with magnitude AND direction, drawn as an arrow, written (a, b).
The length/size of a vector: |(a, b)| = √(a² + b²).
a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ — a single number; 0 means perpendicular.

story.title: The Tug of Directions
story.text: Sana pulls a cart 3 steps east and 4 steps north. 'How far is it in a straight line?' asks Coach. The displacement vector is (3, 4), and its magnitude is √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 steps. 'Direction matters, so we used an arrow — and Pythagoras gave its length,' Coach explains.
story.extractedProblem: Find the magnitude of the vector (3, 4).

visual.caption: A vector is a real arrow with direction and length. |(3, 4)| = √(3²+4²) = 5. Drag to orbit and see it in space.

teachingGallery.title: Length, add, dot
teachingGallery.note: Magnitude by Pythagoras; add components; dot product gives a number.

examples.caption: If two vectors are perpendicular, their dot product is 0.

standardMethod.summary: Magnitude |(a,b)| = √(a²+b²). Add/subtract component by component. Dot product a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ (add a₃b₃ in 3D); it is 0 for perpendicular vectors.
steps: Write the vector in components (a, b) or (a, b, c).
Magnitude: square each component, add, take the square root.
Add or subtract vectors by matching components.
Dot product: multiply matching components and add them.
If the dot product is 0, the vectors are perpendicular.

commonMistakes.mistake: Adding the magnitudes of two vectors instead of adding their components.
commonMistakes.fix: Add vectors COMPONENT by component: (1,2)+(3,4) = (4,6). Only then find a magnitude if needed.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking the dot product is another vector.
commonMistakes.fix: The dot product is a single NUMBER (a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂), not a vector.

easy.q: Add (1, 2) + (3, 4). What is the x-component of the result?

challenge.q: Two vectors have dot product 0. They are…

questions.q: The dot product of two vectors is…

teachBackPrompt: Draw an arrow (3, 4) for a grown-up and show its length is √25 = 5. Add (1,2)+(3,4) = (4,6) component by component, and compute the dot product (1,2)·(3,4) = 11 — a single number.

revisionCard.summary: A vector has magnitude and direction (an arrow), written (a,b). Magnitude = √(a²+b²). Add/subtract component by component. Dot product a·b = a₁b₁+a₂b₂ = a NUMBER; 0 means perpendicular. Add components (not magnitudes); the dot product is a scalar, not a vector.

rememberIt.hook: Vector = arrow with size AND direction. Add tip-to-tail
rememberIt.unpack: Magnitude |(a,b)| = sqrt(a^2+b^2). Add components. Dot product a.b = 0 means perpendicular.

studentQuestions.q: How is a vector different from a number?
studentQuestions.a: A number is just size; a vector also has DIRECTION, like an arrow. Force and velocity are vectors.

studentQuestions.q: What does a dot product of 0 mean?
studentQuestions.a: The two vectors are perpendicular (at right angles).
